q: The elements with low electron affinity easily gain electrons, resulting in the formation of anions. 2024
London dispersion forces are the strongest intermolecular forces. 2024
Many inorganic substances and millions of organic compounds have covalent bonding. 2024
Hydrogen bond is a special type of dipole-dipole interaction that occurs between nonpolar molecules. 2024
Two electrons with the same spin can occupy the same orbital. 2024
Silicon (III) oxide is an example of a giant covalent molecule. 2024
The higher the value of electronegativity, the smaller is the atom's attraction to electrons. 2024
The total dipole moment of CO2 is not zero and it is a non-polar molecule. 2024
Ionic bonding generally occurs between reactive metals and reactive non-metals. 2024

In water molecules, hydrogens are partially positive and oxygens are partially negative. 2025
There is no intermolecular hydrogen bonding between ether molecules. 2025
The ionic compounds are always neutral. 2025
The hydrogen bond is weaker than all other intermolecular forces. 2025
Aufbau principle states that electrons are filled in the higher energy atomic orbitals before filling lower energy ones. 2025

The molecular shape of the ammonia molecule, NH3, is tetrahedral. 2025
Na+ and Cl- ions, have the electronic configuration of the noble gas Ne. 2025
Polar covalent bond is formed between two similar non-metal atoms. 2025
The cubic unit cell can have two types. 2025
A double bond is formed by sharing only one pair of electrons between two atoms. 2025
In ionic compounds, the cations and anions are held together by strong electrostatic attraction. 2025
The hydrogen bond is the weakest of all intermolecular forces. 2025
Any non-polar covalent bond has a zero dipole moment. 2025
Carbon dioxide is a non-polar molecule and the dipole moment is not zero. 2025
Face-centred cubic unit cell is a cubic cell with an atom on each corner, and another in the centre of the cube. 2025
The higher the value of the electronegativity, the greater is the atom's attraction to electrons. 2025
Lewis structure of carbon monoxide molecule consists of two covalent bond and one coordinate bond. 2025
London dispersion forces are the strongest intermolecular forces that can be found in all substances. 2025
In a metallic bond, the valence electrons of the atoms are very loosely held, forming a common electron cloud. 2025

The boiling points of some metals become lower compared with the ionic compounds. 2025
The octet rule does not apply to s-block elements. 2025
Unit cell is the smallest repeating unit of a crystal lattice that shows the three dimensional pattern of the entire crystal. 2025
For an atom, the number of valence electrons is equal to its period number in the Periodic Table. 2026
The electronegativity of bonded atoms and the geometry of molecules play an important role in determining their polarity. 2026
Only valence electrons can take part in chemical bonding. 2026
